full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Filtering out papers that were already selected by other students
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: these paper are done !!! we don want any same paper selection
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Pivoted the search to find unique, unselected papers to avoid the strict duplication penalty.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Checked the live Google Sheet to confirm the previously suggested papers were taken.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Evaluating implementation difficulty of candidate papers
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Since you have read pdf containing all the constraint, and the difficulty to be faced after selecting our paper.. which is easier in between these ? 1.Paper: 'Support Vector Machines as Probabilistic Models' ... 2. 'A Kernel Two-Sample Test' ... 3. 'Laplacian Support Vector Machines Trained in the Primal'
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Provided a comparative analysis of implementation difficulty, highlighting the risks of complex mathematical proofs vs standard library compatibility.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Reviewed the methods to confirm they would be too difficult to implement from scratch on a CPU.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Verifying the provided paper link and catching an LLM hallucination
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: but here. https://www.jmlr.org/papers/volume12/shalev-shwartz11a/shalev-shwartz11a.pdf the name is not Pegasos: Primal Estimated sub-GrAdient SOlver for SVM
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: The LLM hallucinated the publication venue for the Pegasos paper. This prompt caught the error, revealing Pegasos was not published in an allowed CORE A* venue, forcing a pivot to a valid paper.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Checked the actual PDF at the link and verified the true publication venue of Pegasos, confirming it was ineligible.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Addressing the hallucination AFTER the immutable form was submitted
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: what the hell i have already submiitted the form for Timestamp: March 1, 2026... Paper Title: Pegasos...
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Helped me formulate a strategy for radical transparency. The LLM advised me to document this exact hallucination in the JSON to comply with the honest disclosure rule.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified that the exam instructions heavily reward transparency and disclosure of LLM failure modes.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Validating a safe replacement paper against the live tracking sheet
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: now check is Training and Testing Low-degree Polynomial Data Mappings via Linear SVM is in this list or no
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Confirmed that the replacement paper was not taken by any other student, allowing me to safely select it.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Did a final manual search on the live Google Sheet to ensure no duplicates existed before committing to the new paper.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Locating datasets for Part B reproduction
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: now where can i find the dataset for this ppaer
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Identified the exact LIBSVM datasets (like a9a and ijcnn1) used by the authors, ensuring I could replicate the experiments on a local CPU.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Checked the LIBSVM repository link provided and confirmed the datasets were publicly available and properly sized for local execution.

top_5_prompts.prompt: but here. https://www.jmlr.org/papers/volume12/shalev-shwartz11a/shalev-shwartz11a.pdf the name is not Pegasos: Primal Estimated sub-GrAdient SOlver for SVM
top_5_prompts.why_important: This was the most critical prompt because it caught an AI hallucination regarding the publication venue. It prevented me from working on an ineligible paper that would have disqualified me from Parts B and C.

top_5_prompts.prompt: what the hell i have already submiitted the form for Timestamp: March 1, 2026... Paper Title: Pegasos...
top_5_prompts.why_important: This captures the exact moment I realized the LLM hallucinated the publication link and venue for Pegasos after initial submission. It serves as a real-world lesson on the dangers of blindly trusting LLM outputs, directly fulfilling the exam's mandate to critically analyze tool usage.

top_5_prompts.prompt: now check is Training and Testing Low-degree Polynomial Data Mappings via Linear SVM is in this list or no
top_5_prompts.why_important: This prompt finalized my pivot to a valid, CORE A* paper that was strictly compliant with all constraints and confirmed it was safe from the duplication penalty.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Since you have read pdf containing all the constraint, and the difficulty to be faced after selecting our paper.. which is easier in between these ? 1.Paper: 'Support Vector Machines as Probabilistic Models' ... 2. 'A Kernel Two-Sample Test' ... 3. 'Laplacian Support Vector Machines Trained in the Primal'
top_5_prompts.why_important: This focused my selection entirely on reproduction feasibility, ensuring I avoided papers with heavy mathematical proofs that would be a nightmare to code on a standard laptop.

top_5_prompts.prompt: now where can i find the dataset for this ppaer
top_5_prompts.why_important: This prompt secured the logistical requirements for Part B, confirming that the new paper utilized open-source data that I could comfortably run on my machine.

student_declaration.statement: I declare that this JSON file contains a complete and honest record of my LLM usage for Part A, including the explicit documentation of an LLM hallucination regarding a paper's link/venue that I discovered during the selection process.
